explainability.findings: Sources describe documented explainability practices in 1 source(s). Additional documentation references related practices.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Publish user-facing explanation interfaces or documented appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This lab session description provides evidence for **explainability, fairness, governance, privacy, and transparency**. It supports these pillars by discussing AI testing frameworks and benchmarks to ensure unbiased models (fairness), operational processes for measuring AI success and managing risks (governance and transparency), and the need to address bias amplification, misinformation, and privacy risks from LLMs (privacy and fairness). Furthermore, it highlights AI assurance for reliability and transparency in agentic systems (transparency and governance) and mentions foundational AI principles and model capabilities, contributing to transparency about AI systems.
relevant_sources.title: Lab Session: Understanding Generative AI – Foundations & Optimization

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: Documentation discusses AI testing frameworks and benchmarks aimed at ensuring unbiased models. It also references the need to address bias amplification from large language models. Furthermore, blog posts mention AI and bias, indicating a concern for mitigating discriminatory outcomes.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: This blog post, "Deliciously Smart: Exploring the Role of AI in Food Industry," provides evidence for the **fairness**, **governance**, and **privacy** pillars of responsible AI. It supports fairness by mentioning AI and bias, indicating a concern for mitigating discriminatory outcomes. The source also touches on governance by referencing AI/algorithms and challenges like bias and regulatory compliance, implying policy considerations for responsible implementation. Finally, it supports privacy by mentioning AI and privacy concerns related to data for AI, suggesting a policy-level consideration for data protection.
relevant_sources.title: Deliciously Smart: Exploring the Role of AI in Food Industry

governance.findings: Documentation describes operational processes for measuring AI success and managing risks, and highlights AI assurance for reliability in agentic systems. Additionally, blog posts reference AI algorithms and challenges including bias and regulatory compliance.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for **governance** and **transparency** in responsible AI. It supports transparency by explicitly naming the MicroBoost AI tech-engine and detailing its use in Lavie Bio's product development process, documenting the AI systems and their capabilities. The mention of proprietary platforms and the description of the AI tech-engine's focus and application also imply control and decision-making, thus supporting the governance pillar.
relevant_sources.title: Lavie Bio Announces Licensing Agreement for Bio-Fungicides with Corteva Agriscience

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned blog post, "Future of AI in Agriculture," provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The post supports transparency by detailing AI system components like computer vision and facial recognition, explaining their use in monitoring and documenting plant traits, and outlining the intended problem-solving capabilities of the AI system. Implicit governance is suggested through mentions of machine learning algorithms learning, adapting, and making decisions, as well as the description of the AIFARMS initiative and its purpose.
relevant_sources.title: Future of AI in Agriculture

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: Documentation references the need to address privacy risks associated with large language models. Blog posts also mention AI and privacy concerns related to data for AI, suggesting policy-level consideration for data protection.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

transparency.findings: Documentation explicitly names the MicroBoost AI tech-engine and details its use in product development, describing the AI system's capabilities, focus, and application. Published materials also describe operational processes for measuring AI success, highlight AI assurance for reliability in agentic systems, and mention foundational AI principles. Additionally, blog posts detail AI capabilities for analysis, describe software tools for insights, and outline the intended problem-solving capabilities of AI systems, including components like computer vision and facial recognition.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

summary.overall_findings: Corteva's public disclosures detail the use of the MicroBoost AI tech-engine in product development, demonstrating practices in transparency, and describe operational processes for measuring AI success and managing risks, reflecting its approach to governance. These materials address 5 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars, with fairness disclosures discussing AI testing frameworks and benchmarks to ensure unbiased models. Additionally, explainability practices are described in one source, and privacy materials reference the need to address privacy risks from LLMs. No qualifying public evidence was found for oversight or external accountability, based on a review of 14 publicly available sources.
